expected.notes: The one-action schema must ask which separate action to create and must not silently choose a date.

proposal.explanation: The notice contains two explicitly separate actions: submitting an online eligibility form and attending a review appointment. One action cannot be selected without clarification. The form’s due date gives no time of day, so it cannot be represented as a precise ISO 8601 datetime without inventing a time; the appointment date is not an action deadline.

conflicts: The document requires two separate actions: “Submit the online eligibility form by 22 July 2026” and “Attend your review appointment on 24 July 2026 at 10:00.”
The form due date specifies 22 July 2026 but no time of day or offset.
The review appointment date and time is an appointment, not an action deadline.
uncertainty.clarificationQuestion: Which action should be analysed: submitting the eligibility form or attending the review appointment?
